findings.description: u_0002 ('Images housed in the Accent shape — asymmetric rounded-corner crops, not plain rectangles or circles') is a distinct, testable design rule (image framing/cropping shape) but has no corresponding candidate rule. It is currently unextracted, leaving a gap in the Image Treatment cluster.

payload.statement: Images must be housed in the Accent shape (asymmetric rounded-corner crop); plain rectangles or circles are prohibited.

findings.description: Rule is flagged as a hard_constraint compliance requirement ('brand accessibility rule') but no governance facet (owning standard, enforcement body, or review authority, e.g., WCAG reference) is captured, despite the source explicitly calling it out as a governance-tied rule.

payload.governance_note: Tag with accessibility governance facet (e.g., WCAG alt-text compliance, brand accessibility standard owner) per source annotation '(brand accessibility rule)'.

findings.description: Source text is descriptive/aspirational ('Conveys returning to everyday life...') without an explicit imperative, yet rule is tagged polarity=must with hardness=strong_default. This overstates the strength/polarity of the source language; consider softening polarity or confirming strong_default is warranted vs. soft_guidance.
